core_principles: Fusion over Imitation: Mix Executive Polish with Swiss Precision
Emotion First: Trust, Competence, and Availability
Content is King: The Coach is the Product
Contrast: Rhino Navy (#2E3558) vs. Stark White

headings.usage: H1 for Coach Name, H2 for Section Headers

hero.structure: Centered or Split based on viewport. Mobile: Stacked.

elements.name: Avatar
elements.style: w-32 h-32 md:w-48 md:h-48 border-4 border-white/10 shadow-xl rounded-full object-cover

elements.name: Name
elements.style: text-4xl md:text-5xl font-bold tracking-tight text-white mb-2

elements.name: RoleCompany
elements.style: text-xl text-periwinkle font-medium flex items-center gap-2

AvailabilityGrid.container: bg-white rounded-3xl shadow-sm border border-gray-100 p-8 -mt-10 relative z-10 mx-4 md:mx-auto max-w-6xl
AvailabilityGrid.grid: grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-4

slot_card.style: border border-gray-100 rounded-xl p-4 hover:border-periwinkle transition-colors cursor-default bg-slate-50

anti_ai_rules: Do not use gradients for the main background. Use solid #2E3558.
Do not center align large blocks of text. Left align for readability.
Use 3x more padding than you think is necessary.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure white text on Rhino background has sufficient opacity (min 0.9).
accessibility.interactive: All slots must have hover states even if view-only.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Create the page at '/coach/:slug'. 2. Mock data for the coach if backend is not ready, but structure it to receive 'mentor' data. 3. Use the 'HeroSection' designs described above. 4. The availability grid should be the centerpiece - clean, distinct, and easy to read. 5. Ensure the page is responsive (stack on mobile).
